notes: I expected 'be humble, do not chase promotions' coaching content and braced for it. Instead the verse explicitly refuses that frame: 'It is not coaching humility. It is naming a causal chain.' That is the right level. The Rust + pprof + Tokio scenario is a real engineering one — and the framing 'eyes are on the screen, attention is not' captures the actual phenomenology of attention-capture rather than turning it into virtue-discourse. The fix-is-dropping-not-suppressing distinction is sharp and correct: suppression spends more attention; dropping returns it. The diagnostic is checkable (where is your attention currently allocated?) without becoming moralistic. PASS.
